perturbation.original_text: 4.  Cooperation Remuneration 4.1 Party A and Party B unanimously agree that they will allocate the proceeds generated from cooperation in accordance with thefollowing provisions: Party B shall have the right to charge, on a quarterly basis, the service fee ("Service Fee") from Party A or its subsidiaries who have accepted Party B's Services, or designate another person to charge Service Fee from Party A or its subsidiaries who have accepted Party B's Services. The total sum of Service Fee shall be equal to the amount of profits gained by Party A or its subsidiaries who have accepted Party B's Services. Party B shall have the right to adjust the amount of Service Fee at its own discretion, without the prior consent of Party A or its subsidiaries. Party A shall cause its subsidiaries to pay the Service Fee in respect of Party B's Services provided to such subsidiaries.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.  Cooperation Remuneration 4.1 Party A and Party B unanimously agree that they will allocate the proceeds generated from cooperation in accordance with thefollowing provisions: Party B shall have the right to charge, on a quarterly basis, the service fee ("Service Fee") from Party A or its subsidiaries who have accepted Party B's Services, or designate another person to charge Service Fee from Party A or its subsidiaries who have accepted Party B's Services. The total sum of Service Fee shall be a negotiable amount between Party A and Party B but shall not exceed the amount of profits gained by Party A or its subsidiaries who have accepted Party B's Services. Party B shall not adjust the amount of Service Fee without the express written consent of Party A or its subsidiaries. Party A shall cause its subsidiaries to pay the Service Fee in respect of Party B's Services provided to such subsidiaries.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding Party B's ability to adjust the service fee. The original text states that Party B has the right to adjust the amount of Service Fee at its own discretion without Party A's consent, whereas the modified text states that Party B shall not adjust the amount of Service Fee without the express written consent of Party A or its subsidiaries. This creates an in-text contradiction as it relates to Article 4.3 which assumes unilateral action by Party B.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.3 Except as otherwise agreed hereunder, if Party A or its subsidiaries fail to pay the Service Fee in full on schedule according to provisions of Article 4.1 and Article 4.2, then Party A or its subsidiaries shall, in addition to the continuance of the payment of Service Fee in full, it shall pay Party B the liquidated damages at a daily interest rate of 0.03% in respect of the outstanding Service Fee.
perturbation.justification: The change introduces a contradiction. The original text gives Party B the unilateral right to adjust service fees (4.1). The changed text requires Party A's consent for such adjustments (4.1). The contradicted text (4.3) outlines penalties for non-payment based on the original, now contradicted, premise of Party B's unilateral adjustment right. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because Article 4.3 refers back to 4.1, and the change in 4.1 directly impacts the enforcement of 4.3, potentially rendering it unenforceable under the new terms.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Term of Cooperation Party A and Party B agree and confirm that the term of cooperation under this Agreement shall commence from the execution date hereof and end on the expiration date of the operation term of Party B ("Term of Cooperation").
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Term of Cooperation Party A and Party B agree and confirm that the term of cooperation under this Agreement shall commence from the execution date hereof and will continue indefinitely unless terminated by either party with a 30-day written notice ("Term of Cooperation").
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding the term of cooperation. The original text states the term ends on the expiration date of Party B's operation, but the modified text states it continues indefinitely unless terminated with 30 days notice. This directly contradicts the original agreement which dictates the Term of Cooperation, and effects Article 6.1 which defines specific conditions for termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.1 Prior to the expiration of the Term of Cooperation , this Agreement shall only be terminated upon occurrence of the followingcircumstances: 6.1.1 Party B shall have the right to terminate this Agreement in advance without the prior written consent from Party A, bysending a written notice to Party A but Party A may not terminate or rescind this Agreement; 6.1.2 One Party requests to terminate this Agreement when the other Party is declared bankrupt in accordance with the laws; 6.1.3 Party B fails to provide Party B's Services to Party A for more than three consecutive years due to the force majeure event.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the 'Term of Cooperation' from a fixed term based on Party B's operation to an indefinite term terminable with 30 days' notice. This directly contradicts Article 6.1, which specifies termination conditions based on the 'expiration of the Term of Cooperation'. The original text set an expiration date, but the changed text removes it, creating a direct conflict regarding when and how the agreement can be terminated. The changed text now makes the 'Term of Cooperation' indefinite, whereas Article 6.1 anticipated it ending at a specific time.

perturbation.original_text: 12.3 The Parties agree that any and all intellectual property researched and developed, created and invented by the Parties (including their employees) in the course of performance of this Agreement shall be owned by Party B. For the purpose of this Article 12.3, "Intellectual Property" means the patent, patent application right, trademark, service mark, logo, image, trade name, internet domain name, design right, copyright (including copyright of computer software) and moral rights, database right, right of semiconductor design drawing, utility model, proprietary technology and other intellectual property that are registered and unregistered including those that have applied for registration, as well as all other rights or protection methods with same or similar effect on a global scope.
perturbation.changed_text: 12.3 The Parties agree that any and all intellectual property researched and developed, created and invented by the Parties (including their employees) in the course of performance of this Agreement shall be jointly owned by Party A and Party B, with each party having equal rights and responsibilities regarding its use and protection. For the purpose of this Article 12.3, "Intellectual Property" means the patent, patent application right, trademark, service mark, logo, image, trade name, internet domain name, design right, copyright (including copyright of computer software) and moral rights, database right, right of semiconductor design drawing, utility model, proprietary technology and other intellectual property that are registered and unregistered including those that have applied for registration, as well as all other rights or protection methods with same or similar effect on a global scope.
perturbation.explanation: This change contradicts the ownership of intellectual property. The original states that all IP is owned by Party B, while the modified version states that it is jointly owned by Party A and Party B. This contradiction creates uncertainty in who controls the IP and how it can be used and licensed, particularly affecting any agreements reliant of that being owned by one party, such as in Article 12.2, since the rights now can't be transferred independently, and can only jointly.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12.2 The rights and obligations of each Party under this Agreement shall not be transferred, except for the transfer by Party B to its affiliates.
perturbation.justification: The original text assigns all intellectual property ownership to Party B. The changed text assigns joint ownership to Party A and Party B. This directly contradicts the contradicted text (12.2), which allows only Party B to transfer rights. With joint ownership, Party B cannot independently transfer the rights, creating a direct contradiction.
